methodology.description: Conversion rates derived from industry-specific overall rate applied to traffic source base rates

source.title: eCommerce Statistics and Benchmarks by Industry - XP2
source.snippet: Beauty & Personal Care: 5.74% overall conversion rate (200M+ users, 300M+ sessions)

derivation.method: Industry overall rate × paid traffic base ratio

conv_paid.notes: Derived: industry overall 5.74% (DY) adjusted for paid traffic which typically converts at 0.83× overall

source.title: eCommerce Statistics and Benchmarks by Industry - XP2
source.snippet: Beauty & Personal Care: 5.74% overall conversion rate (200M+ users, 300M+ sessions)

derivation.method: Industry overall rate × organic traffic base ratio

conv_organic.notes: Derived: organic/direct traffic typically converts at 1.0× overall rate

source.title: eCommerce Statistics and Benchmarks by Industry - XP2
source.snippet: Beauty & Personal Care: 5.74% overall conversion rate (200M+ users, 300M+ sessions)

derivation.method: Industry overall rate × email/retention traffic base ratio

conv_retention.notes: Derived: email/SMS traffic converts at 2.5× overall; high repeat purchase category

source.title: Customer Acquisition Cost Benchmarks by Industry
source.snippet: Beauty and cosmetics brands see average CAC of $40-50

cac_paid.notes: Varies significantly by sub-category (skincare higher, makeup lower)

aov_typical.notes: DTC skincare tends higher ($80-100), cosmetics lower ($50-70)

source.title: Understanding Margins and Revenue in DTC Ecommerce
source.snippet: Beauty/cosmetics DTC brands typically achieve 50-60% gross margins

gross_margin_typical.notes: High margins due to perceived value, low COGS for formulations
